author: @DDDProjects
text: Getting more and more frustrated with my AD5x. I have about 250 hours, since the first release. If the nozzle doesn't clog the filament jams in the extruder. It will print well for a about 3 days and then I spend a week trying to fix it. I hope the new design works better. It would be great if they would upgrade or have some path for current owners to get the "new" parts.

author: @cartercordingley6062
text:
I have been using gcode to swap colors.  And I have prints that taken a week. Why Because it has over 200 filament swaps and it waiting for me to swap it and I sleeping or working.  
I just bought the AD5X and is waiting for it to ship out can't wait to see how fast it will now print

author: @Forensic1Man
text: Rob, Do you have a pet bird in the house?  You need to introduce him on camera and possibly 3D print a bird toy!!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - I live near a country park mate - the birds are wild birds in the garden

author: @thesupremeparadox
text: You could print inserts for the cardboard spools?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yeah I think could - nice idea mate thanks for sharing

author: @slrnuttall
text: Thanks for the update mate! Love the printing sounds in the background, sounds like peeved birds.  Cheers!

author: @Dr-dikhead
text: Hope all is well Robert! Never stop inspiring and creating! Cheers.

author: @12thsonofisrael
text:
Nice machine 👏 

And thank you for the coupon.

I wish reviewers went into the cheap quality of the SBC and electronics at the back. It's the components most likely to fail, but it's hidden and most reviewers don't have the expertise to look at it. These printers are half computer nowadays.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: At the end of the day it's like anything - how well it stands up and how well it does the job you want it to do - This thing is 6 months old still going strong and as you know I use it a lot - That's all I can say about it really - but it is also all that matters.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - could well be mate cheers

author: @BillsWorkbench
text: I agree, when I started 3d printing that was the hobby, now it’s part of my larger hobby.  I like to make parts for my RC cars and trucks, my best investment last year was upgrading to the Bambu P1S.  Now I print for my hobby and not make a hobby out of printing.

author: @silverpc4611
text: With the printer being so open. I am surprised that you don't have any problems with bed adhesion. I can see that it might be an easy fix using some card board and zip ties.

replies.author: @SirTodd
replies.text: it's designed so you can 3d print your own side panels or add your own however you want

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I get what you mean - open frame and all - but all I can say is my experience of it

replies.author: @danp1224
replies.text: Set the tip a bit closer. i was having heaps of problems so one day i made it a bit tighter and not had a problem since.
